tips: Use console.table() to display arrays and objects in a readable table format
console.time() and console.timeEnd() measure execution time of code blocks
Use debugger statements or browser DevTools breakpoints for step-by-step debugging
Log the full error object — console.error(err) is better than err.message alone
Use try/catch around JSON.parse and other fallible operations
Check network requests with the Network tab when debugging API issues
Reproduce bugs with minimal, isolated test cases before fixing
Add meaningful log context — include variable names alongside values
Use source maps to debug transpiled or minified code
Set custom log levels (info, warn, error, debug) to filter noise
Use Bun's --inspect flag to attach Chrome DevTools for debugging
Structured logging with JSON makes log parsing and querying easier
